title: Life in colour
subtitle: Workbook practice: adjective suffixes and pronouns

blocks.title: Vocabulary · Adjective suffixes
blocks.text: Complete exercises 1a and 1b. Make adjectives from the words in brackets.

blocks.title: Adjective suffixes
blocks.text: Use these suffixes: -able / -ible, -ate, -ive, -ous, -ful. Example: ATTRACT → attractive.

blocks.title: 1a. Complete the sentences with the adjective form
blocks.instructions: Use a suffix from the list. Type only the missing adjective.

items.prompt: I think Charlie’s very ______ — he always looks great! (ATTRACT)
My aunt is a very ______ woman. She volunteers at a hospital every weekend. (COMPASSION)
My girlfriend is very ______. She isn’t afraid to express her opinion. (ASSERT)
It was very ______ of you to visit me in hospital. (THOUGHT)
Sue’s a ______ child. We all adore her. (LOVE)
I’m very ______ of my friend’s new car. (ENVY)
She’s a ______ author. She’s written many books and won several important prizes. (SUCCESS)
Jess is a ______ teenager. She often looks after the neighbour’s children. (RESPONSE)
My sister is rather ______. She doesn’t think before she acts. (IMPULSE)
My nephew is really ______. He often does my shopping for me. (HELP)
Philip was quite ______ in his teens, but now he’s calmed down. (REBEL)
You have to be careful what you say to Helen. She’s very ______. (SENSE)

blocks.title: 1b. Make adjectives from the nouns and verbs
blocks.instructions: Use each word from the list once. Type only the missing adjective.

items.prompt: Maria’s children are very ______. They always give us a big hug when we arrive. (AFFECTION)
Matt’s girlfriend is very ______. She doesn’t let him talk to other girls. (POSSESS)
My boss is quite ______. She always lets me take time off if my children are ill. (CONSIDER)
My assistant isn’t very ______. I can’t trust her to do anything important. (RELY)
Lisa’s very ______. She looks like an actress! (GLAMOUR)
He’s a very ______ businessman. He owns several large companies and he makes a lot of money. (POWER)
Anna’s really ______. She’s made some wonderful sculptures. (CREATE)

blocks.title: Grammar · Pronouns
blocks.text: Complete exercises 3a, 3b, and 3c. Pay attention to object pronouns and the word order with to / for.

blocks.title: 3a. Complete the sentences with a pronoun or possessive adjective

items.prompt: My wife and I are having a baby girl. We’re going to call ______ Eloise.
I’d like you to meet my new boyfriend. ______ name’s Tom.
items.explanation: Use the possessive adjective his before the noun name.

items.prompt: I’m staying at my sister’s house. I always stay with ______ when I go to the UK.
items.explanation: After with, use the object pronoun her.

items.prompt: We aren’t going to Emma and Ian’s wedding. They haven’t invited ______.
items.explanation: After invited, use the object pronoun us.

items.prompt: Can I borrow your dictionary, please? I’ve forgotten ______.
I have two nephews. ______ names are Richard and Matthew.
items.explanation: Use the possessive adjective their before names.

items.prompt: We go everywhere by public transport. The bus stops right outside ______ house.
items.explanation: Use the possessive adjective our before house.

items.prompt: Do you know where my keys are? I can’t find ______ anywhere.
items.explanation: Keys is plural, so use them.

items.prompt: My car is nearly out of petrol. Let’s take ______.
It’s my dad’s birthday tomorrow. I mustn’t forget to call ______.
items.explanation: After call, use the object pronoun him.

blocks.title: 3b. Choose the correct word order
blocks.instructions: Choose the correct ending for each sentence.

items.prompt: The children love that book. My husband reads…
They gave…
I’ll send… when I arrive.
items.explanation: I’ll send you a message when I arrive.

items.prompt: What do you think of my curtains? My mum made…
items.explanation: My mum made them for me.

items.prompt: She showed…
items.explanation: She showed me her holiday photos.

items.prompt: I’m going to lend…
items.explanation: I’m going to lend her a suitcase.

items.prompt: Do you like my new phone? My cousin sold…
items.explanation: My cousin sold it to me.

items.prompt: Put on these gloves. I bought…

blocks.title: 3c. Complete the answers using pronouns and to / for
blocks.instructions: Change the word order. Type only the missing part after the subject.

items.prompt: A: Who showed you the photo? B: Jack ______.
A: Who sent Sophie those flowers? B: Her husband ______.
items.explanation: Her husband sent them to her.

items.prompt: A: Who’s going to buy your girlfriend a car? B: Her father ______.
items.answer: is going to buy it for her
acceptedAnswers: is going to buy it for her
's going to buy it for her
items.explanation: Her father is going to buy it for her.

items.prompt: A: Who sold your parents their new car? B: Their neighbour ______.
items.explanation: Their neighbour sold it to them.

items.prompt: A: Who makes your lunch? B: My mum ______.
items.explanation: My mum makes it for us.

items.prompt: A: Who gave you and your husband that picture? B: A colleague ______.
items.explanation: A colleague gave it to us.

items.prompt: A: Who’s going to get you and your friends the tickets? B: My brother ______.
items.answer: is going to get them for us
acceptedAnswers: is going to get them for us
's going to get them for us
items.explanation: My brother is going to get them for us.

items.prompt: A: Who’s reading Max the story? B: His grandmother ______.
items.explanation: His grandmother is reading it to him.
